articles.title: Trump Dismisses a Merger While Seeking Suitors for Spirit Airlines
articles.summary: As he rejected the idea of a merger of United Airlines and American Airlines, President Trump said someone, including possibly the federal government, should buy or help Spirit.

articles.title: Tim Cook Was Very, Very Good at Making Money
articles.summary: If the Steve Jobs era was defined by technological innovation, the Tim Cook period was one of exceptional financial growth.

articles.title: She raised concerns about her company's contracts with ICE. Then she lost her job
articles.summary: Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz May Not Return to Normal, Whether It’s Open or Closed
articles.summary: The energy industry is planning for a future where the choke point on Iran’s southern coast is a lot less important.

articles.title: Supreme Court Reviews F.C.C.’s Enforcement Power Against Communications Companies
articles.summary: AT&T and Verizon were penalized millions of dollars for what the agency said was a failure to protect consumer information. The companies say they were deprived of their right to a jury trial.

articles.title: Europe Wanted a Say on the Iran War, but It’s Still on the Sidelines
articles.summary: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give the continent a role. But Tehran and Washington are still calling the shots.

articles.title: Want to lighten your mental load? First, let go of these gender myths
articles.summary: "Men can't see the mess." "Women are better at chores." These myths position women to take on more emotional thinking, says researcher Leah Ruppanner. She shares what works to reclaim your headspace.

articles.title: A Year After U.S.A.I.D.’s Death, Fired Workers Find Few Jobs and Much Loss
articles.summary: People have plowed through savings, cashed out retirement funds and moved in with relatives. Former U.S.A.I.D. workers estimate that less than half have found full-time work.

articles.title: As the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline looms, here are the main sticking points for talks
articles.summary: The status of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear enrichment program are among the biggest obstacles to reaching a deal.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
articles.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing

articles.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends Wednesday
articles.summary: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends Wednesday. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

articles.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
articles.summary: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to un

articles.title: A Wish List for John Ternus, the Man Replacing Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
articles.summary: John Ternus, an Apple veteran who runs hardware engineering, will take over an extraordinarily profitable company in need of new ideas.

articles.title: ‘Wagyu’ Used to Guarantee Quality Beef. What Are You Paying for Today?
articles.summary: Behind the scenes, competing forces battle for the reputation of prestige meat.

articles.title: The surprising origin of 4 features that superglue kids — and adults — to screens
articles.summary: Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.

articles.title: 3 things to know about Trump's pick to head the Fed as Senators weigh his nomination
articles.summary: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve went before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.

articles.title: Japan approves removing ban on lethal weapons exports
articles.summary: Japan endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a significant change of its postwar pacifist policy, as the country seeks to build up its arms industry.

articles.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
articles.summary: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.

articles.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
articles.summary: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

articles.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
articles.summary: Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.

articles.title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports
articles.summary: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
